text:
Once upon a time, in a quirky little town named Giggleswick, every summer afternoon featured the annual Fruit Pie Festival. People from all over came to munch on pies and sing karaoke battles. The highlight was always the pie-eating contest, where the biggest enthusiasts would chomp their way through towering stacks of fruit pies.

This particular year, Maria entered. Known throughout Giggleswick as the pie-loving park ranger, she had won last year. This year, however, something inside her was chirping away. It told her she deserved a pie more than anyone.

Amidst a sea of giggling contestants and judges, Maria stepped up to the plate. She cracked open a pie that wasn't quite her favorite kind, but she wolfed it down.

The judge called her name. Her face split into a grin. She closed her eyes, tasted the sweetness, remembered the chirpy chirp echoing through the trees the previous summer, and confidently declared, "I like my
